tool.name: windowmasker
tool.description: NCBI WindowMasker genome-frequency repeat/low-complexity intervals as content-annotated BED6
tool.command: set -o pipefail && #if str($input.ext) == 'fasta.gz' gunzip -c '$input' #else cat '$input' #end if | awk '/^>/{print;next}{print toupper(\$0)}' > in.fa && windowmasker -mk_counts -in in.fa -out counts && windowmasker -ustat counts -in in.fa -outfmt interval | awk -f '$__tool_directory__/interval2bed.awk' | python3 '$__tool_directory__/lc_classify.py' in.fa - | LC_ALL=C sort -k1,1 -k2,2n > '$output'
tool.help: **WindowMasker** (NCBI BLAST+) learns over-represented k-mers from the genome itself (no repeat library needed — well suited to Plasmodium) and masks repeats/low-complexity. Two-pass (``-mk_counts`` then ``-ustat``). Emitted as **BED6** with per-interval content: name = repeat-unit signature, score = purity x1000, strand = ``.``. Input is upper-cased first so masking is computed de novo.
